title: caroselinum chabraei
intro: Caroselinum chabraei, also known as Chabra's carrot or carrot-root, is a type of flowering plant in the carrot family that is native to the Himalayan region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Caroselinum chabraei include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a valuable herb for treating various ailments. Caroselinum chabraei can be prepared in various herbal forms, including dried roots, powders, and infusions, and is often used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
